skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Apps: x/post-tweet, x/post-create (with media), x/post-like, x/post-retweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow. Capabilities: post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles. Use for: social media automation, content scheduling, engagement bots, audience growth, X API. Triggers: twitter api, x api, tweet automation, post to twitter, twitter bot, social media automation, x automation, tweet scheduler, twitter integration, post tweet, twitter post, x post, send tweet
skill.summary: Automate Twitter/X posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inference-sh-8

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 48 potential issues, but evaluation reveals all are false positives. External command detections are markdown documentation examples, not actual execution. Network URLs are legitimate inference.sh service endpoints. The pipe-to-shell install method uses SHA-256 verification as documented mitigation. This is a standard Twitter API client skill with acceptable risk for marketplace publication.

high_findings.title: Pipe to Shell Installation
high_findings.description: The CLI installation uses curl | sh pattern to install the inference.sh CLI. While common for software installation, piping downloaded scripts to shell carries inherent risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl | sh pattern is detected, but documentation notes SHA-256 checksum verification as a mitigation. This is a standard install method for many CLI tools.

content.value_statement: Automate your Twitter/X workflow with posting, likes, retweets, DMs, and user following through the inference.sh CLI. Perfect for social media managers and content creators.
actual_capabilities: Post tweets with media images or videos
Get user profiles and tweet details
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and API credentials
Rate limits depend on Twitter/X API tier
Cannot schedule posts directly (must use external scheduling)

use_cases.title: Social Media Manager
use_cases.description: Automate posting content across multiple Twitter accounts, like competitor posts for engagement, and follow relevant users.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers handling brand accounts

use_cases.title: Content Creator
use_cases.description: Generate AI images or videos and automatically post them to Twitter with captions.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators using AI generation tools

use_cases.title: Developer Integrations
use_cases.description: Build custom Twitter workflows by chaining the inference.sh CLI with other AI apps.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building social media automation tools

prompt_templates.title: Post Simple Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to post a tweet that says: {tweet_text}

prompt_templates.title: Post Tweet with Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to post a tweet with the image at {media_url} and caption: {caption}
prompt_templates.scenario: Posting a tweet with an image attachment

prompt_templates.title: Engage with Post
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to like the tweet at {tweet_url} and then follow the author
prompt_templates.scenario: Liking a post and following the author

prompt_templates.title: AI Image to Twitter Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: First generate an image using the falai/flux-dev-lora app with prompt: {image_prompt}, then use twitter-automation to post it with caption: {caption}
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating an AI image and posting to Twitter

output_examples.input: Post a tweet saying 'Just launched my new product!'
output_examples.output: The skill runs: infsh app run x/post-tweet --input '{"text": "Just launched my new product!"}' and returns confirmation with tweet ID and metadata.

output_examples.input: Like the tweet from @OpenAI about their latest announcement
output_examples.prompt: First get the tweet ID, then like it
output_examples.output: The skill retrieves the tweet ID and executes: infsh app run x/post-like --input '{"tweet_id": "..."}' confirming the like was successful.

best_practices: Always review tweet text before posting to avoid typos or unintended content
Space out automated actions to respect Twitter rate limits
Use Twitter developer credentials with appropriate permission levels
anti_patterns: Do not use for spam or aggressive automated engagement that violates Twitter terms
Avoid posting the same content repeatedly without variation
Do not attempt to automate actions faster than rate limits allow

faq.question: Do I need a Twitter developer account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to set up Twitter/X API credentials through inference.sh to use this skill.

faq.question: Can I schedule tweets in advance?
faq.answer: This skill posts immediately. For scheduling, use an external scheduler or cron job to trigger the post at desired times.

faq.question: Is this safe for business accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, when used within Twitter API rate limits and terms of service. Always review automated content before posting.

faq.question: What happens if Twitter rate limits me?
faq.answer: The API will return an error. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff or reduce automation frequency.

faq.question: Can I delete tweets with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, use the x/post-delete app with the tweet_id to delete a tweet.

faq.question: Does this work with X Premium features?
faq.answer: Feature availability depends on your Twitter API tier. Basic posting works with free tier.
